recommendations.description: Merge 2 duplicate rule(s) checking same fields

critical_issues.description: Rule checks KeyManager equals AWS (which means AWS-managed key) but doesn't check actual public accessibility configuration. AWS-managed keys are not publicly accessible, but this is an indirect check.

cross_service_analysis.note: Note: All methods used are kms service methods
cross_service_analysis.explanation: All python_methods (describe_key, list_keys, list_aliases, list_grants, get_key_rotation_status, get_key_policy) belong to kms service. No cross-service issues found.
cross_service_analysis.recommendation: No action needed - rules correctly use kms API methods

yaml_structure.field_paths: All field paths match emit structure

state_change_monitoring_enabled.issue: Rule checks item.CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn but this field is not available in KMS describe_key API response
state_change_monitoring_enabled.metadata_intent: Should check that CloudWatch logging is enabled for KMS key state changes
state_change_monitoring_enabled.reason: KMS logging is configured through CloudTrail, not through a direct field in the describe_key response. The field CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn does not exist in the KMS API.
state_change_monitoring_enabled.recommendation: This rule will always fail because the field doesn't exist. KMS logging should be checked through CloudTrail configuration, which would require cross-service checks.

logging_rules.description: Several logging-related rules check KeyManager/KeyId/Enabled instead of actual CloudWatch logging configuration
logging_rules.reason: KMS logging is configured through CloudTrail, not through direct fields in the KMS API response. These rules use placeholder checks.
logging_rules.recommendation: These rules should check CloudTrail configuration for KMS logging, which would require cross-service checks.

certificate_expiration.issue: Rule only checks KeyManager equals CUSTOMER, doesn't check actual certificate expiration date
certificate_expiration.reason: Certificate expiration information may not be available in the standard describe_key response, or may require additional API calls
certificate_expiration.recommendation: Review AWS KMS API documentation to determine if certificate expiration fields are available. If not, this rule may need to be renamed or the check may need to be performed differently.

tls_rules.description: TLS-related rules check KeyManager/Enabled instead of actual TLS version configuration
tls_rules.reason: TLS version configuration may not be exposed in the KMS API response, or may be enforced at the service level rather than per-key
tls_rules.recommendation: Review AWS KMS API documentation to determine if TLS version fields are available. If not, these rules may need to be renamed or the checks may need to be performed differently.

lifecycle_policy.issue: Rule only checks KeyManager/Enabled/KeyState, doesn't check actual lifecycle policy configuration
lifecycle_policy.current_implementation: Checks item.KeyManager equals CUSTOMER, item.Enabled equals true, item.KeyState equals Enabled
lifecycle_policy.reason: Lifecycle policy information may not be available in the standard describe_key response, or may require additional API calls
lifecycle_policy.recommendation: Review AWS KMS API documentation to determine if lifecycle policy fields are available. If not, this rule may need to be renamed or the check may need to be performed differently.
